description_long: This house features a distinct Tudor architectural style, characterized by its steeply pitched gable roof, prominent stone chimney, and half-timbered facade with dark wooden beams and light infill panels. The front elevation displays a symmetrical arrangement of large, multi-pane windows, while the side elevations reveal a mix of window sizes and placements. The roof is covered in gray slate tiles, and the stonework extends up the chimney and lower walls, adding a sense of solidity. The structure appears to have three stories, with each level marked by rows of windows and timber framing. The entryway is understated, blending into the facade without a prominent porch or steps. Overall, the house exudes a historic, medieval English charm suitable for a traditional urban neighborhood.
description: A three-story Tudor-style house with a steep gable roof, half-timbered facade, and prominent stone chimney, featuring symmetrical rows of multi-pane windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical half-timbered facade, three gabled roof peaks, and a large stone chimney on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the house, the central gable, and the stone chimney extending up the side wall.
description_view_2: Rear view with similar half-timbered detailing and window arrangement, but less prominent chimney features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view dominated by the tall stone chimney and a more solid wall with fewer windows.
attribute_entryway_design: Subtle entryway integrated into the facade, no prominent porch or steps, framed by timber and stone elements.
